findings.description: Cover page layout differs - HTML renders content in a more linear/flat layout without the centered title block styling of the PDF
findings.evidence: PDF has a bold, centered title block with horizontal rule above it; HTML renders it more plainly with left-aligned content

findings.description: Professional engineer seal/stamp is missing or significantly different in HTML version of cover page
findings.evidence: PDF shows a North Carolina Professional Engineer seal (038141) with DocuSigned signature; HTML shows similar but placement differs

findings.description: Table of Contents layout slightly different - HTML uses more vertical spacing between entries
findings.evidence: PDF has compact TOC with dotted leaders; HTML has more spread out entries without visual dotted leaders

findings.description: Section headings in the body text lose underline formatting in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows underlined section headings like '2.1 Outfall 002 Sample Types'; HTML renders them without underlines

findings.description: Italic text in CO Addendum quoted passage formatting differs
findings.evidence: PDF shows the CO Addendum requirements in italic text; HTML preserves italics but block indentation is slightly different

findings.description: Mathematical equations rendered differently - HTML uses inline text formatting instead of centered equation display
findings.evidence: PDF shows Equation 1 as centered mathematical notation with summation symbol; HTML renders as inline subscript text 'M_OF002 = Σ^N_n=1 m_OF002,n − m_intake,n'

findings.description: Table 1 formatting differs slightly between PDF and HTML but data content is preserved
findings.evidence: PDF shows table with tighter formatting; HTML shows same data in a wider table format with different cell styling

findings.description: Figure 1 satellite/aerial image is present in HTML but rendered with text labels extracted as separate text elements
findings.evidence: PDF shows integrated map figure with embedded labels; HTML shows the image with some label text extracted below

findings.description: Figure 2 flowchart - HTML renders the flowchart decision boxes and content as plain text instead of the graphical flowchart
findings.evidence: PDF shows a full graphical flowchart with diamond decision nodes and rectangular outcome boxes; HTML extracts just the text content as a linear sequence (YES/NO, decision questions, outcomes)

findings.description: Figure 3 flowchart - HTML renders the flowchart content as plain text instead of the graphical flowchart
findings.evidence: PDF shows graphical flowchart for River Water Intake concentration determination; HTML extracts text linearly

findings.description: Footer information and page numbering consolidated/removed in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF has 'Outfall 002 Mass Load' footer with page number and date on each page; HTML shows these inline or omits them

findings.description: Multiple h1 headings used. The document has 'SITE CONVEYANCE NETWORK AND OUTFALL 002 PFAS MASS LOADING CALCULATION PROTOCOL' as h1, then 'TABLES' as h1, and 'FIGURES' as h1. Only one h1 should be used for the document title.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains:

SITE CONVEYANCE NETWORK AND OUTFALL 002 PFAS MASS LOADING CALCULATION PROTOCOL

,

TABLES

,

FIGURES

findings.description: Inconsistent heading hierarchy. Section 1 'INTRODUCTION' and Section 2 'OUTFALL 002 AND RIVER INTAKE SAMPLING' are rendered as h2, but Section 3 'PFAS MASS LOADING CALCULATION METHODOLOGY' is also h2. However, the TABLE OF CONTENTS, LIST OF TABLES, LIST OF FIGURES, and LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS are rendered as h3, when they are document-level headings that should be at the same level or higher than subsections.
findings.evidence: TABLE OF CONTENTS is <h3>, LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS is <h3>, but subsections like 2.1 Outfall 002 Sample Types are also <h3>

findings.description: The Table of Contents is reproduced in full as text on the page, in addition to the actual content sections themselves. This mirrors the PDF structure and is acceptable, but the TOC page header content is duplicated with the actual Geosyntec logo appearing repeatedly as embedded images throughout.
findings.evidence: Multiple

findings.description: In Table 1, all rows share the same 'Analytical Method' value of 'Table 3+ Lab SOP'. In the PDF, the Analytical Method column appears as a single merged cell spanning all rows. The HTML representation repeats this value per row, which is an acceptable interpretation of a merged cell but technically creates redundant data.
findings.evidence: PDF shows single merged cell 'Table 3+ Lab SOP' spanning all analyte rows; HTML repeats 'Table 3+ Lab SOP' 12 times

findings.description: In Equation 1, the summation notation in the HTML loses the structure of the original equation. The PDF shows the subtraction of m_intake,n occurring inside the summation, but the HTML text representation is ambiguous about this.
findings.evidence: PDF: M_OF002 = Σ(n=1 to N) m_OF002,n − m_intake,n. HTML: 'M<sub>OF002</sub> = ∑<sup>N</sup><sub>n=1</sub> m<sub>OF002,n</sub> − m<sub>intake,n</sub>'

findings.description: The HTML includes a file path reference 'Illustrator/Flow Charts/OutfallProtocol_flowchart_Sec3.4.ai' and 'Illustrator/Flow Charts/OutfallProtocol_flowchart_Sec3.5.ai' as text content. These appear to be extracted from the PDF figure source metadata and are present in the PDF page content, so not truly fabricated.
findings.evidence: These paths appear in the PDF pages 16 and 17 as part of the figure reference metadata
